2606.09811.title: AHA-WAM:Asynchronous Horizon-Adaptive World-Action Modeling with Observation-Guided Context Routing
2606.09811.paper_summary: World-action models have emerged as a promising paradigm for robot manipulation, jointly modeling visual scene dynamics and actions to inject physical priors into policy learning. However, existing world-action models couple world prediction and action execution at the same temporal resolution, forcing the world branch to model near-term frame variations that are redundant and weakly informative. We posit that strictly binding world prediction and action execution to the same temporal rhythm may underutilize the potential of the video branch for embodied control. Therefore, we propose AHA-WAM, an Asynchronous Horizon-Adaptive World-Action Model built on a dual Diffusion Transformer (DiT) architecture that reorganizes world-action modeling around this temporal asymmetry. AHA-WAM instantiates the video DiT as a low-frequency world planner that maintains rolling key-value memory over past observations and exposes reusable layerwise latent context encoding long-horizon scene evolution, while a high-frequency action DiT executes short action chunks in closed loop by querying this context through layerwise joint attention. To support asynchronous execution, we introduce horizon-adaptive offset training and Observation-Guided Video-Context Routing (OVCR), which together let the action expert exploit long-horizon world context while remaining responsive to real-time execution state without rerunning the video DiT. Experiments on RoboTwin and real-world manipulation tasks show that AHA-WAM achieves state-of-the-art performance without any robot-data pretraining, attaining 92.80% average success on RoboTwin and 78.3% success across 4 real-world tasks, while reaching 24.17 Hz closed-loop control with a 4.59x speedup over Fast-WAM.
2606.09811.paper_summary_zh: 世界行動模型已成為機器人操作的一個有前景的範式，聯合建模視覺場景動態和行動，以將物理先驗注入策略學習中。<lb/>然而，現有的世界行動模型在相同的時間解析度下耦合世界預測和行動執行，迫使世界分支建模近短期幀變化，這些變化是冗餘且信息量弱的。<lb/>我們認為，將世界預測和行動執行嚴格綁定在相同的時間節奏上可能會未能充分利用視頻分支在具身控制中的潛力。<lb/>因此，我們提出了AHA-WAM，一種非同步地平線自適應世界行動模型，基於雙重擴散Transformer（DiT）架構，圍繞這種時間不對稱重新組織世界行動建模。<lb/>AHA-WAM 將視頻 DiT 實例化為一個低頻世界規劃器，該規劃器保持對過去觀察的滾動關鍵值記憶，並暴露可重用的逐層潛在上下文編碼長期場景演變，而高頻行動 DiT 通過逐層聯合注意查詢這一上下文，在閉環中執行短行動片段。<lb/>為了支持非同步執行，我們引入了地平線自適應偏移訓練和觀察引導視頻上下文路由（OVCR），這兩者共同使行動專家能夠利用長期世界上下文，同時對實時執行狀態保持響應，而無需重新運行視頻 DiT。<lb/>在 RoboTwin 和現實世界操作任務上的實驗顯示，AHA-WAM 在沒有任何機器人數據預訓練的情況下達到了最先進的性能，在 RoboTwin 上達到 92.80% 的平均成功率，在 4 個現實世界任務中達到 78.3% 的成功率，同時以 24.17 Hz 的閉環控制達到比 Fast-WAM 快 4.59 倍的速度提升。
2606.09811.author: Jisong Cai et.al.
2606.09811.authors: Jisong Cai, Long Ling, Shiwei Chu, Zhongshan Liu, Jiayue Kang, Zhixuan Liang, Wenjie Xu, Yinan Mao, Weinan Zhang, Xiaokang Yang, Ru Ying, Ran Zheng, Yao Mu
